items.title: Per-Harness Override Layers
items.description: Allow users to define harness-specific overrides that survive sync cycles. Today, syncing overwrites any manual customizations made in Codex/Gemini/OpenCode. With override layers, users can say 'Gemini always adds these extra rules' or 'Codex strips this MCP server' — persistent exceptions that get merged on every sync. Solves the biggest adoption blocker: users afraid to customize target harnesses because sync will nuke their changes.

items.title: Dry-Run Diff Preview
items.description: Before any sync operation, show a rich colored diff of exactly what will change in each target harness — added lines, removed lines, modified sections. Currently users sync blind and discover surprises after the fact. A preview command makes sync feel safe and builds trust, especially for first-time users or before major config changes.

items.title: Git Branch-Aware Sync Profiles
items.description: Automatically switch sync profiles when the user checks out a git branch. On 'feature/new-client' branch, sync a client-specific rule set. On 'main', sync the default config. Hooks into post-checkout git hooks. Solves context-switching overhead for developers who work on multiple projects or clients from the same machine.

items.title: Migrate-From Wizard
items.description: Guided wizard that reads an existing Gemini/Codex/OpenCode config and migrates it into Claude Code as the new source of truth. Detects conflicts, maps native concepts to Claude Code equivalents, and shows what will be lost or approximated. Critical for users who started on another harness and want to consolidate — currently they must manually reconstruct their entire config.

items.title: Team Config Sync via Git
items.description: Designate a git repo or subdirectory as the shared source of truth for a team. Team members pull shared rules, skills, and MCP configs automatically. Changes proposed via PRs, reviewed, merged, then broadcast to all team members on next sync. Solves the 'we all set up Claude Code differently and get different results' problem that plagues team standardization efforts.

items.title: Installed Harness Auto-Discovery
items.description: On first run and periodically, scan the system for installed AI coding CLIs (checking PATH, common install locations, package managers). Auto-suggest adding newly discovered harnesses to the sync targets. Removes the manual 'I installed Windsurf last week but forgot to configure it' problem — HarnessSync stays current with what's actually installed.

items.title: Selective Sync Filters
items.description: Sync only specific config categories to specific targets: 'sync only MCP servers to Codex', 'sync rules but not skills to Gemini', 'sync everything to OpenCode'. Today it's all-or-nothing per target. Users with partial overlap between harnesses — e.g. using Gemini only for quick questions, not full projects — need fine-grained control over what crosses the boundary.

items.title: MCP Server Compatibility Report
items.description: Analyze each configured MCP server and report which target harnesses support it, which partially support it, and which will ignore it. Include version requirements, transport type compatibility, and known workarounds. MCP adoption is accelerating but compatibility is a mess — users waste hours discovering post-sync that their Playwright MCP doesn't work in Codex.

items.title: Config Health Score Dashboard
items.description: Score each harness's config on dimensions: completeness (what Claude Code features are represented), freshness (how long since last sync), coverage (% of source config successfully mapped), and fidelity (how well the mapping approximates the original). Surface the lowest-scoring harness and actionable improvements. Gives users a clear answer to 'how well am I actually synced?'

items.title: Community Adapter Builder
items.description: Interactive wizard to scaffold a new harness adapter: prompts for config file locations, format (JSON/TOML/Markdown), supported feature set, and generates a skeleton adapter with test fixtures. Lowers the barrier to community contributions and lets power users add support for niche tools (Continue.dev, Cody, Avante.nvim) without needing to understand HarnessSync internals.

items.title: Harness Version Compatibility Warnings
items.description: Detect the installed version of each target harness and warn when a config feature requires a newer version than what's installed. 'This MCP transport type requires Gemini CLI >= 0.4.0, you have 0.3.1.' Currently users get silent failures or confusing errors in the target harness. Version-aware warnings turn mysterious failures into actionable upgrade prompts.

items.title: Portable Config Bundle Export
items.description: Export the entire Claude Code config as a self-contained archive (ZIP or tarball) with all skills, rules, MCP configs, and a manifest. Importable on any machine to bootstrap HarnessSync. Solves new machine setup and laptop migration — currently users must manually recreate their entire config environment when switching machines.

items.title: Auto-Generated Sync Changelog
items.description: After each sync, generate a human-readable changelog of what changed and why: 'Added 3 MCP servers (file-system, github, postgres). Updated 12 rules from CLAUDE.md revision. Removed skill: old-debugging (deleted from source).' Stored locally and optionally committed to git. Gives users a clear audit trail and makes it easy to understand the history of their harness configs.

items.title: Cross-Harness Permission Audit
items.description: Generate a report showing what each harness is allowed to do: file system access, shell execution, network calls, tool allowlists. Highlight discrepancies where Claude Code is more permissive or restrictive than targets. Security-conscious teams need this to ensure a skill that's sandboxed in Claude Code isn't accidentally running unconstrained in Codex.

items.title: Cursor / Windsurf / Aider Adapters
items.description: Expand sync targets beyond Codex/Gemini/OpenCode to cover Cursor (.cursorrules, .cursor/mcp.json), Windsurf (cascade rules), and Aider (.aider.conf.yml, conventions). These are the three most-used alternatives to Claude Code among AI developers. Unlocks HarnessSync for the majority of the target audience who uses at least one of these tools.

items.title: Cross-Harness Skill Smoke Tests
items.description: For each synced skill, run a lightweight smoke test on each target harness to verify the skill loads and executes a basic invocation without errors. Flag skills that fail on specific targets. Developers invest heavily in skills — discovering that a skill silently broke on Gemini three syncs ago is infuriating. Automated smoke tests catch breakage early.

items.title: Universal Config Search
items.description: Search across all harness configs simultaneously for a keyword, rule text, MCP server name, or skill content. Returns matches with harness, file, and line context. When debugging why a particular behavior differs between harnesses, users currently must manually open 4-5 config files. Universal search answers 'where is this defined and in which harnesses?' instantly.

items.title: Drift Detection Alerts
items.description: Detect when target harness configs have been manually modified outside of HarnessSync and alert the user at session start: 'Gemini CLI config was modified 2 days ago outside of sync — 3 rules may have drifted.' Calculates a drift score and surfaces it prominently. Prevents the silent config rot that builds up when users forget which tool is source of truth.

items.title: Interactive First-Run Onboarding
items.description: A guided checklist-driven setup that walks new users through: detecting installed harnesses, choosing sync targets, configuring accounts (for multi-account setups), running a first sync, and verifying each target. Today's setup is manual and documentation-dependent. A guided onboarding reduces time-to-first-sync from hours to minutes.

items.title: MCP Server Discovery & Install
items.description: Browse a curated list of popular MCP servers (filesystem, GitHub, Postgres, Playwright, etc.) with one-command install that adds them to Claude Code config AND immediately syncs to all harnesses. Currently users must find MCP servers, manually configure them, then remember to sync. A discovery layer makes MCP adoption frictionless.

items.title: Git Commit Hook Integration
items.description: Option to install a git pre-commit or post-commit hook that syncs config automatically whenever CLAUDE.md or .claude/ files change as part of a commit. Ensures harness configs are always in sync with the codebase's Claude config, especially useful for project-scoped configs checked into version control. Zero-effort sync for teams who store configs in git.

items.title: Capability Gap Advisor
items.description: Analyze what Claude Code features you're using that have no equivalent in target harnesses, and suggest workarounds or alternatives: 'You use 4 hooks that Codex doesn't support — here's how to approximate this behavior with Codex's native event system.' Turns incompatibility from a dead end into actionable guidance, helping users get maximum value from secondary harnesses.

items.title: Config Annotations & Comments
items.description: Add inline annotations to rules, skills, or MCP entries that explain intent, source, or sync behavior: 'This rule is Claude Code only — not synced to Codex because it relies on hook behavior.' Annotations survive sync cycles and appear in relevant harness configs where supported. Eliminates the mystery of 'why does this rule exist and where did it come from?'

items.title: Scheduled Background Sync
items.description: Configure sync to run on a schedule (hourly, daily, on login) without needing Claude Code to be open. Runs as a lightweight background process or launchd/systemd service. For users who keep Claude Code open all day this is less critical, but for users who open different tools at different times, scheduled sync ensures configs never go stale.

items.title: Multi-Project Workspace Manager
items.description: Manage sync for multiple distinct project directories from a single HarnessSync instance. Switch between projects with a command, see sync status across all projects, and push global rules to all workspaces simultaneously. Power users juggling 5+ projects today must run sync commands from each project directory — workspace management gives them a single pane of glass.

items.title: Rule Source Attribution Tracking
items.description: Track where each synced rule originated: which CLAUDE.md file, which skill, which line number. Display this provenance in the sync report and in target configs as comments. When a rule causes unexpected behavior in a target harness, users need to trace it back to its source quickly — attribution turns debugging from archaeology into a direct lookup.

items.title: Incremental Sync Engine
items.description: Replace full-config rewrites with patch-based incremental sync that only modifies changed sections of target configs. Reduces sync time, reduces risk of corrupting manually-managed sections of target configs, and makes diff output more meaningful. For large configs with many MCP servers and skills, full rewrites are slow and risky.

items.title: Secret & Env Var Leak Scanner
items.description: Before syncing, scan all source configs for patterns that look like API keys, tokens, or secrets accidentally embedded in rules or MCP configs. Block sync and warn the user. Env var references are safe; literal secrets are not. As MCP configs grow to include auth tokens and credentials, accidental secret propagation across harness configs is a real security risk.

items.title: Rollback with Before/After Preview
items.description: Enhance the existing rollback command to show a before/after diff for each target before executing. Let users selectively roll back only specific harnesses or config sections rather than a full rollback. Current rollback is all-or-nothing and blind — users are afraid to use it. Surgical rollback with preview makes it a tool people actually reach for.

items.title: Starter Config Template Library
items.description: Curated starter templates for common developer personas: 'Full-Stack Web Dev', 'Data Scientist', 'DevOps Engineer', 'Open Source Maintainer'. Each template includes relevant rules, recommended MCP servers, and skills tuned for that use case — all immediately synced across harnesses. Eliminates the blank-slate problem for new users who don't know where to start.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolution
items.description: When sync detects that a target config has been manually modified AND the source has changed, present an interactive 3-way merge interface showing source, target, and last-synced state. Let users choose per-section: take source, keep target, or manually merge. Today, sync silently overwrites or silently skips — neither is acceptable when configs represent hours of tuning.

items.title: VS Code AI Extension Config Sync
items.description: Extend sync to cover VS Code AI extension configs: GitHub Copilot instructions (.github/copilot-instructions.md), Continue.dev config, and Cody settings. VS Code remains the dominant editor and its AI extensions have their own config formats. Developers who use Claude Code in terminal + Copilot in VS Code currently maintain two separate rule sets — this unifies them.

items.title: PR Sync Summary Comment
items.description: When a PR modifies Claude Code config files (CLAUDE.md, .claude/), automatically post a comment showing what will change in each target harness when the branch is merged. Reviewers see the downstream impact of config changes in the PR itself. Teams using config-as-code practices need visibility into 'what does merging this PR actually change for every team member's tools?'

items.title: Always-On File Watcher Daemon
items.description: A persistent background daemon that watches Claude Code config files for changes and syncs immediately — no hook needed, no manual trigger. Works even when Claude Code isn't running. Install once, forget forever. The current PostToolUse hook only fires during active Claude Code sessions, leaving configs stale during long sessions in other tools.

items.title: Named Sync Profiles
items.description: Save and switch between named sync profiles: 'work', 'client-acme', 'personal', 'security-audit'. Each profile has its own set of target harnesses, sync filters, and config sources. Switch profiles with a single command. Consultants and freelancers who work under different contexts with different rules and tool permissions today maintain completely separate machine setups.

items.title: Live Harness Feature Matrix
items.description: A dynamically updated compatibility matrix showing which features of each harness HarnessSync supports, pulled from a maintained registry. Includes version-specific notes and links to workarounds. Users currently discover feature gaps by trial and error. A live matrix sets accurate expectations before users invest in configuring a harness.

items.title: Skills Marketplace Integration
items.description: Browse and install community-contributed Claude Code skills directly from within HarnessSync, with automatic propagation to all configured harnesses. Rate and review skills. Currently, finding and installing skills requires manual searching and copying — a marketplace with one-command install + instant sync dramatically lowers the barrier to extending any harness.

items.title: Config Size & Token Optimizer
items.description: Analyze CLAUDE.md and rules for redundancy, verbosity, and duplication — then suggest compressed equivalents that preserve intent. Show estimated token savings per harness. Large CLAUDE.md files slow context loading and cost tokens on every session. An optimizer that respects user intent while reducing bloat has direct cost and performance impact.

items.title: MCP Tool Stub Generation for Non-MCP Harnesses
items.description: When syncing to harnesses without MCP support, auto-generate shell script stubs that approximate MCP tool behavior (e.g., a bash script that wraps the same underlying API). Users who rely on MCP tools in Claude Code get at least degraded-but-functional equivalents elsewhere.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.

items.title: MCP Tool Compatibility Matrix
items.description: Show which MCP tools work in which harnesses and flag tools that will silently fail. Users waste hours debugging why an MCP capability works in Claude Code but not in Gemini.
